summary: Control route descriptor values belong to extracted core. FastAPI process behavior, mutable block-control runtime state, and effect interpreter dispatch do not belong to extracted core and remain visible for downstream owner issues.

pure_core_successors.reason: The family asserts closed route-set names, route methods, paths, scopes, descriptors, configurable prefix construction, and fail-closed lookup. These are protocol values, not a web-framework implementation.

downstream_handoffs.reason: The family asserts FastAPI routing, optional dependency behavior, token enforcement, request body bounds, ordinary route separation, and authenticated runtime mutation semantics. Those are process/API laws, not pure core descriptor values.

The family asserts mutable in-process runtime state, target replacement, observer mutation, status/log provider callbacks, and runtime contract backing. Core already owns pure control contracts; mutable state and callbacks belong to operations or cpk-server process composition.
The family asserts dispatch to capability-specific effect interpreters and rejection of misadvertised interpreters. That is interpreter registry behavior, not the pure route descriptor language.

laws: FastAPI, ASGI, request parsing, optional server dependencies, and HTTP process auth do not enter extracted core.
Mutable block-control state does not enter extracted core.
Effect interpreter dispatch does not enter extracted core.
Pure control-route values remain in extracted core as closed descriptors.
